records.ety: [OE. kissen, cussen, AS. cyssan, fr. coss a kiss; of uncertain origin; akin to D. kus, G. kuss, Icel. koss.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Kissed (kst);p. pr. & vb. n. Kissing.]

defs: To salute with the lips, as a mark of affection, reverence, submission, forgiveness, etc.
quotes: He . . . kissed her lips with such a clamorous smack, That at the parting all the church echoed.

defs: To touch gently, as if fondly or caressingly.
quotes: When the sweet wind did gently kiss the trees.

defs: To make or give salutation with the lips in token of love, respect, etc.; as, kiss and make friends.
To meet; to come in contact; to touch fondly.
quotes: Like fire and powder, Which as they kiss consume.
Rose, rose and clematis, Trail and twine and clasp and kiss.

records.ety: [OE. kiss, derived under the influence of the verb from the older form coss, AS. coss. See Kiss, v.]

defs: A salutation with the lips, as a token of affection, respect, etc.; as, a parting kiss; a kiss of reconciliation.
quotes: Last with a kiss, she took a long farewell.
Dear as remembered kisses after death.
